title: uragoga cornuta
intro: Uragoga cornuta, also known as the "Tropical Bitter Apple," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. Uragoga cornuta is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Uragoga cornuta can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
